title: Non-invasive detection of the backflow of urine
abstract: The present relates to a method, system and a device for non-invasive detection of urine flow from the bladder into the kidney(s). The method, system and device rely on measurements made at distinct time points and can be used to detect Vesicoureteral reflux. The method, system and device are designed to detect changes in urine volume in the ureter(s), bladder and / or kidney(s). The method and device measure conductivity changes by bioelectrical impedance or electrical impedance tomography technology.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A system for detecting urine flowing from the bladder to the kidney(s) and / or ureter(s) in a subject using bioelectrical impedance or electrical impedance tomography technology, the system comprising an impedance measurement module configured to record impedance measurements in the kidney(s) and / or ureter(s), and a processor configured to receive data from the impedance measurement module the data being impedence measurements taken over a period of time, and the processor being further configured to analyse the differences between measurements or groups of measurements to provide an indication of volume changes in the kidney. 2. The system as claimed in claim 1 wherein the impedance measurement module comprises a plurality of electrodes adapted to record impedance measurements, the electrodes being adapted to be placed in conductive contact or to be capacitively coupled with the skin and being connectable to a control unit. 3. A system for measuring urine flow from the bladder to the kidney(s) or ureter(s) as claimed in claim 1 or 2 wherein the processor is configured to produces graphs or images to provide an indication of volume of urine in the kidney over a period of time. 4. A system as claimed in any preceding claim wherein measurements are taken as bladder pressure increases during micturition or filling, or as bladder pressure increases as pressure is applied to the abdomen and hence to the bladder, either manually or by the system itself. 5. The system as claimed in any preceding claim wherein data is collected during micturition and compared to a baseline measurement taken when no micturition is occurring. 6. The system as claimed in claim 5 wherein the data collected is plotted as graphs or shown as images. 7. The system as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the impedance measurement module comprises at least two electrodes which may be placed in contact with the skin and are connectable to a control unit to sense impedance changes. 8. The system as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the impedance measurement module comprises a unit comprising electrodes the unit being in direct conductive contact or capacitively coupled to the surface of the body and a control unit that is detachable from the unit. 9. The system of any preceding claim wherein the processor is integrated into or connectable with a user interface or display unit. 10. The system of any preceding claim wherein the electrodes are adapted when in use to be positioned on the body surface adjacent to the area of interest i.e. the bladder, ureter(s) or kidney(s) of the patient. 11. The system of any preceding claim further comprising means for securing the electrodes to the body selected from a quick release fastener, an elastic strap, a harness securable around the thighs and/or shoulders or neck, a bib, brace, sling or halter. 12. The system as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the control unit and electrode unit are attachable directly to a diaper or shorts comprising absorbable material. 13. The system as claimed in any preceding claim further comprising a wetness indicator, and/or a humidity or temperature sensor. 14. The system of any preceding claim further comprising an oscillator adapted to indicate if excessive movement of the patient has resulted in noise that would impact the accuracy of the results. 15. The system of any preceding claim wherein the unit comprising the electrodes incorporates an adhesive or high friction material to prevent movement of the electrodes. 16. The system of any preceding claim in which micturition is detected using a button or switch incorporated into the system that may be used to create a time-stamp in the data. 17. The system as claimed in any preceding claim further comprising a means to gather 'clean catch' samples of urine during measurement to allow urine culture analysis to diagnose a urinary tract infection and to determine the volume of urine passed. 18. A method for detecting urine flowing from the bladder to the kidney(s) or ureter(s) in a subject comprising recording conductivity changes occurring in the ureters, bladder and/or kidneys which indicate changes in volume in the ureters, bladder and / or kidney wherein conductivity changes are detected by bioelectrical impedance or electrical impedance tomography and these volumes being compared to control values to indicate the presence or absence of VUR in the test subject. 19. A method as claimed in claim 18 wherein measured voltage changes are used to construct images or graphs that indicate changes in volume of urine in the ureters, bladder and / or kidney (s). 20. A method as claimed in claim 18 or claim 19 wherein measurements are taken as bladder pressure increases during micturition or filling, or as bladder pressure increases as pressure is applied to the abdomen and hence to the bladder, either manually or by the system itself. 21. A method as claimed in any of claim 18 to 20 wherein data is collected during micturition and compared to a baseline measurement taken when no micturition is occurring. 22. A method as claimed in any one of claim 18 to 21 wherein metrics including maximum conductivity change and max-to-mean ratio are extracted from images. 23. A method as claimed in any of claims 18 to 22 wherein the volume of urine travelling to the kidneys is inferred from the residual volume of urine left in the bladder following urination. 24. A device for detecting urine flowing from the bladder to the kidney(s) in a subject using bioelectrical impedance technology or electrical impedance tomography, wherein said device comprises an impedance measurement module configured to record impedance 

 measurements in the kidney(s) or ureter(s), and a processor configured to receive data from the impedance measurement module over a period of time, and the processor being further configured to analyse the differences between the measurements or groups of measurements to provide an indication of volume changes in the processor over a period of time. 25. The device as claimed in claim 24 wherein the impedance measurement module comprises a plurality of electrodes adapted to detect impedance changes, the electrodes being adapted to be placed in contact with the skin and being connectable to a control unit.
